does: This paper documents Endava's practical implementation of OpenAI's Codex to transform their organizational structure into an agentic system. The work demonstrates how Codex integrates into software development workflows to automate code generation and requirements analysis, achieving dramatic efficiency gains. Specifically, Endava reduced requirements analysis time from weeks to hours and accelerated overall software delivery cycles. The implementation serves as a case study showing how large language models can be systematically deployed across an organization's development processes, with Codex handling code synthesis, documentation generation, and initial architectural decisions that previously required extensive human analysis and planning.
connects: This work extends recent applications of large language models in software engineering, building on developments in neural code generation and AI-assisted programming tools. It responds to ongoing efforts in the field to move beyond single-point tool adoption toward organization-wide AI integration, similar to work exploring AI's impact on software development productivity and quality. The paper engages with broader research on automating requirements gathering and specification—traditionally labor-intensive tasks—by applying transformer-based models trained on code to extract and formalize requirements. It also connects to literature on organizational change driven by automation and how teams adapt when routine analytical work becomes machine-tractable.
enables: This work opens directions for scaling agentic organizations beyond Endava's implementation, suggesting how other enterprises can restructure around AI capabilities rather than fitting AI into existing processes. It points toward fuller automation of the software development lifecycle, from initial requirements capture through deployment and maintenance. The dramatic time reduction enables exploration of how organizations might reallocate freed human expertise toward higher-level architectural decisions, quality assurance, and novel problem domains. It also raises questions about skill requirements, team composition, and knowledge management in AI-augmented development, while potentially creating templates for applying similar agentic approaches in other knowledge-intensive industries facing requirements analysis bottlenecks.
